advisories.statement: delegation.schema.md contains three additive text loci (the scope_lock_ref JSON field, an extended may_mutate rule sentence explaining that field, and the serialization-disclosure line), whereas the plan §3 and build evidence describe 'two additions'. All three are on-topic to the authorized CF13d/scope_lock_ref illustration, within the contract-class allow-row, and validator-neutral (selftest delegation 41/0) — faithful to intent; only the count framing undersells the third sentence.

The chain sub-gate's delegations branch invokes `dmc delegation check --run RID` without `--root`, resolving the run dir via the tool's own repo_root(). Correct for the designed usage (real closure at repo root; copy-surface E2E) and fails closed on the common mismatched-root case; a cross-repo invocation with a colliding content-addressed RID is the only theoretical false-PASS and is implausible. Passing --root would remove the residual ambiguity — a hygiene candidate, not blocking.

The chain sub-gate is honestly a provenance/accountability tier, not tamper-detection: a DELETED delegations.jsonl + DELETED authorization yields chain PASS-with-note (run-dir append-log exemption), and the WAUTH-MISSING-AUTH floor is proven at apply-time (g8c direct apply-check) rather than through the release-time sub-gate. This is explicitly disclosed in the readiness schema and was dispositioned at r2 (Rev 2/A2); the mutation floor remains diff-scope + Ring-1 postbash. Noted for the human release gate's awareness, not a defect.
